cascade_form: Option D-style: dase 28×28 image-caption cos sim → two-threshold pair partition; Stage1 CTAS uncertain (left_id,right_id); Stage2 staging-driver JOIN x product_selection twice (verbatim prefilter), AI.IF prompt verbatim; merge confident_pos ∪ bq_returned

calibration.method: AI.GENERATE_BOOL on Q9 image-pair prompt + thinking_budget=0

baseline.sql:
WITH product_selection AS (
  SELECT images.*
  FROM fashion_product_images.STYLES_DETAILS styles_details
  JOIN fashion_product_images.IMAGE_MAPPING mapping
    ON styles_details.styleImages.default.imageURL = mapping.link
  JOIN EXTERNAL_OBJECT_TRANSFORM(TABLE `fashion_product_images.IMAGES`, ['SIGNED_URL']) as images
    ON ARRAY_LAST(SPLIT(images.uri, '/')) = mapping.filename
  WHERE true
    AND styles_details.baseColour IN ('Black', 'Blue', 'Red', 'White', 'Orange', 'Green')
    AND styles_details.colour1 = ''
    AND styles_details.colour2 = ''
    AND price < 800
)

SELECT
  CONCAT(
    ARRAY_FIRST(SPLIT(ARRAY_LAST(SPLIT(p1.uri, '/')), '.')),
    '-',
    ARRAY_FIRST(SPLIT(ARRAY_LAST(SPLIT(p2.uri, '/')), '.'))
  ) AS id
FROM product_selection p1
LEFT OUTER JOIN product_selection p2
  ON p1.uri != p2.uri
  AND AI.IF(
    ('''
     Determine whether both images display objects of the same category
     (e.g., both are shoes, both are bags, etc.) and whether these objects
     share the same dominant surface color. Disregard any logos, text, or
     printed graphics on the objects. There might be other objects in the
     images. Only focus on the main object. Base your comparison solely on
     object type and overall surface color.''', p1.ref, p2.ref),
    connection_id => 'us.connection',
    endpoint => 'gemini-2.5-flash'
  )

cascade.method: Option D-style: dase 28×28 image-caption cos → two-thresh partition → Stage1 staging pairs → Stage2 verbatim AI.IF on uncertain

stage2_run.sql:
WITH product_selection AS (
  SELECT images.*
  FROM fashion_product_images.STYLES_DETAILS styles_details
  JOIN fashion_product_images.IMAGE_MAPPING mapping
    ON styles_details.styleImages.default.imageURL = mapping.link
  JOIN EXTERNAL_OBJECT_TRANSFORM(TABLE `fashion_product_images.IMAGES`, ['SIGNED_URL']) as images
    ON ARRAY_LAST(SPLIT(images.uri, '/')) = mapping.filename
  WHERE true
    AND styles_details.baseColour IN ('Black', 'Blue', 'Red', 'White', 'Orange', 'Green')
    AND styles_details.colour1 = ''
    AND styles_details.colour2 = ''
    AND price < 800
)

SELECT
  CONCAT(
    ARRAY_FIRST(SPLIT(ARRAY_LAST(SPLIT(p1.uri, '/')), '.')),
    '-',
    ARRAY_FIRST(SPLIT(ARRAY_LAST(SPLIT(p2.uri, '/')), '.'))
  ) AS id
FROM fashion_product_images.q9_uncertain_pairs pairs
JOIN product_selection p1
  ON CAST(ARRAY_FIRST(SPLIT(ARRAY_LAST(SPLIT(p1.uri, '/')), '.')) AS INT64) = pairs.left_id
JOIN product_selection p2
  ON CAST(ARRAY_FIRST(SPLIT(ARRAY_LAST(SPLIT(p2.uri, '/')), '.')) AS INT64) = pairs.right_id
WHERE p1.uri != p2.uri
  AND AI.IF(
    ('''
     Determine whether both images display objects of the same category
     (e.g., both are shoes, both are bags, etc.) and whether these objects
     share the same dominant surface color. Disregard any logos, text, or
     printed graphics on the objects. There might be other objects in the
     images. Only focus on the main object. Base your comparison solely on
     object type and overall surface color.''', p1.ref, p2.ref),
    connection_id => 'us.connection',
    endpoint => 'gemini-2.5-flash'
  )
